sources_consulted: Project Canopy repository design pack, evaluator source, and the existing clean-room showcase and biome-concept assets
General silhouette conventions of stylised low-poly vegetation: exaggerated trunk taper, flared base, and compound foliage cards

risks: These are art-direction candidates rendered by the diagnostic CPU preview, not approved World Studio catalogue assets.
Crown symmetry depends on the manifest seed because bough azimuth is a random draw; seeds were chosen from a per-species sweep and will need re-picking if bough counts or placement modes change.
Final in-engine material tuning, wind authoring, and impostor/LOD baking remain intentionally out of scope.

task.objective: Author and render a low-poly cartoon tree concept family — three large canopied trees plus medium and small companions — as standalone samples with stills and turntable animations.

validation.result: All seven documents valid: zero errors and zero warnings.
All seven exports succeeded with no warnings; draft-profile meshes range from 261 to 3,192 triangles (499 to 7,850 at the production profile).

validation.command: find samples/cartoon/documents -type f -print0 | sort -z | xargs -0 sha256sum | sha256sum
validation.result: Two consecutive generations produced aggregate SHA-256 4ecfee76e3f127557bd73d0fc31574c03437b184c9546f9c42b9d250b2ab0474.

Seven hero stills, seven 18-frame turntable GIFs, two contact sheets and one scale lineup written to samples/cartoon/renders/; each output visually reviewed. Palette-indexed encoding (64-colour GIF, 256-colour PNG) takes the sample directory from 7.1 MB to 3.9 MB with no visible difference against the truecolour renders.
